meds.whatItDoes: Lowers blood sugar in adults with type 2 diabetes. It helps your body use insulin better and tells the liver to make less sugar.
meds.howToTake: Swallow with a meal and a full glass of water. Most people take it once or twice a day at the same times each day.
seriousSideEffects: Stomach pain that does not go away

caution: Heavy alcohol use can raise the risk of side effects

meds.source: DailyMed · U.S. National Library of Medicine

meds.whatItDoes: Lowers blood pressure and helps the heart pump more easily. It belongs to a group of medicines called ACE inhibitors.
meds.howToTake: Swallow with water at the same time each day. It can be taken with or without food. The first dose may make you a little dizzy, so sit or lie down for a while after.
seriousSideEffects: Swelling of the face, lips, tongue, or throat

caution: Foods very high in potassium such as bananas and orange juice if eaten in large amounts
Alcohol can lower blood pressure too much

meds.source: DailyMed · U.S. National Library of Medicine

meds.whatItDoes: Lowers high cholesterol and lowers the chance of a heart attack or stroke. It is one of a group of medicines called statins.
meds.howToTake: Swallow with water once a day. It can be taken any time of day, with or without food. Many people take it in the evening because the body makes the most cholesterol overnight.
seriousSideEffects: Severe or unexplained muscle pain or weakness
Yellowing of the skin or eyes

caution: Alcohol may raise the risk of liver problems
avoid: Grapefruit and grapefruit juice — they can raise the level of this medicine in your blood

meds.source: DailyMed · U.S. National Library of Medicine

meds.whatItDoes: Relaxes blood vessels so the heart does not have to work as hard. It is used for high blood pressure and chest pain (angina).
meds.howToTake: Swallow with water once a day at the same time. It can be taken with or without food.
commonSideEffects: Swelling of the ankles or feet

caution: Grapefruit juice in large amounts may slightly raise the level in your blood

meds.source: DailyMed · U.S. National Library of Medicine

meds.whatItDoes: Replaces the thyroid hormone your body is missing. It is used when the thyroid gland does not make enough hormone on its own.
meds.howToTake: Swallow with a full glass of water on an empty stomach, ideally 30 to 60 minutes before breakfast. Take it at the same time every day.
commonSideEffects: Hair thinning during the first months
Trouble sleeping if dose is too high

caution: High-fiber meals near the dose can lower how much medicine your body absorbs
avoid: Coffee, calcium supplements, iron supplements, soy, and antacids within four hours of the dose — they block absorption

meds.source: DailyMed · U.S. National Library of Medicine

meds.whatItDoes: Thins the blood to help prevent dangerous blood clots, strokes, and heart attacks. It needs regular blood tests to keep the dose right.
meds.howToTake: Swallow with water once a day at the same time. It can be taken with or without food. Never skip blood tests — the dose may need to change often.
seriousSideEffects: Blood in urine or black stools

safe: Most regular meals if your overall diet stays steady
caution: Leafy green vegetables such as spinach, kale, and broccoli are high in vitamin K — you do not need to avoid them, but keep your daily intake about the same so the dose stays right
avoid: Sudden big changes in how much vitamin K food you eat

meds.source: DailyMed · U.S. National Library of Medicine

meds.whatItDoes: A daily small dose helps prevent blood clots in some people who have had a heart attack or stroke, when a doctor has told them to take it. Only use it if your doctor has told you to.
meds.howToTake: Swallow with a full glass of water and a small snack to protect your stomach. Take it at the same time each day.
seriousSideEffects: Vomiting that looks like coffee grounds
Sudden weakness, slurred speech, or trouble seeing

caution: Alcohol can raise the risk of stomach bleeding

meds.source: DailyMed · U.S. National Library of Medicine

meds.whatItDoes: Lowers the amount of acid your stomach makes. It is used for heartburn, acid reflux, and stomach ulcers.
meds.howToTake: Swallow whole with water 30 to 60 minutes before a meal, usually breakfast. Do not crush or chew the capsule.
seriousSideEffects: Severe rash, blisters, or peeling skin

safe: Most regular meals after the dose

meds.source: DailyMed · U.S. National Library of Medicine
